hypothesis: If generative models are pre-trained on large molecular databases (ChEMBL or ZINC), fine-tuned on a curated Covalent Triazine Framework (CTF) dataset, and further optimized with reinforcement learning using expert-defined structural reward functions, then they will generate a higher proportion of valid, novel, internally diverse CTF-like molecules that pass expert structural filters (flatness, symmetry, conjugation, steric criteria) and show improved Fréchet ChemNet Distance and uniqueness metrics compared to models lacking these transfer learning and reward-guidance steps because pre-training, fine-tuning, and targeted rewards steer the models toward the desired CTF chemical space.
